title: Critical Functions Observatory — defensive UAS / critical-infrastructure research lab

fields.vision: A defensive UAS and critical-infrastructure research observatory built around national critical functions rather than generic drone security.
fields.mechanism: It routes malicious-UAS and infrastructure risk through a function-first defensive-research model: CISA National Critical Functions, FAA Remote ID, White House guidance, DOJ/DHS/FAA/FCC legal-advisory constraints, OT asset inventory, DOE/EPA-relevant function dependencies, semantic graphs, provenance vaulting, publication forge outputs, and recomputable projection logic. It also guards against turning defensive analysis into operational attack guidance by filtering timing, payload, and bypass details.
fields.built_components: v1 of the page is build-grade — three of three passes complete, with 29 files at 29 KB compressed (52 KB uncompressed). Five architecture documents, four checklists, five operational templates, five workflow specs, three schema files (edge / node / edge-types catalog), two reference documents (reference register + source hierarchy), and two import seed files (seed_nodes.jsonl + seed_edges.jsonl) — plus the page charter, manifest, map, scoring model, handoff status, index, and README. Project P1 is closed; P2 through P6 remain open as the active research program. The page's explicit out-of-scope items: generating target lists, identifying site-specific weak points, deriving attack routes / timing / payload logic, bypass guidance, transforming defensive analysis into operational attack planning. The seven formal doctrines are what make those exclusions enforceable rather than aspirational.
fields.source_utilization: Source use is organized by role: method authorities, domain evidence, technical background, implementation evidence, and claim limits. The page should show why each source matters; direct quotations and stronger factual claims still require exact passage anchors before publication.
fields.plan: The forward path is to graduate the five remaining open projects (P2 through P6) through the five workflows, populate the semantic graph with real research, and expose recomputable projections to the Publication Forge for external use. Step 01 P2 — first open project First of the five open projects worked through the full five-workflow pipeline (capture — review — modeling — synthesis — monitoring) under the seven doctrines. Step 02 Semantic-graph population Twelve object types populated with real research data; ten relationship types exercised; the graph becomes the active system of record. Step 03 P3-P6 graduation Remaining four open projects worked through the same workflow pipeline; cross-project relationships exercised in the graph. Step 04 Publication Forge live Recomputable projections published as briefings or advisories — the test is that the projection can be regenerated from the graph at any time without re-doing the research.
fields.status: Status: v1 of the page is build-grade — three of three passes complete, with 29 files at 29 KB compressed (52 KB uncompressed). Five architecture documents, four checklists, five operational templates, five workflow specs, three schema files (edge / node / edge-types catalog), two reference documents (reference register + source hierarchy), and two import seed files (seed_nodes.jsonl + seed_edges.jsonl) — plus the page charter, manifest, map, scoring model, handoff status, index, and README.
fields.remaining_gaps: Remaining gaps: the forward path is to graduate the five remaining open projects (P2 through P6) through the five workflows, populate the semantic graph with real research, and expose recomputable projections to the Publication Forge for external use. Next: P2 — first open project First of the five open projects worked through the full five-workflow pipeline (capture — review — modeling — synthesis — monitoring) under the seven doctrines.
fields.detailed_source_vision: A lossless, append-only, function-first defensive-research page for malicious-UAS threats to critical infrastructure — framed entirely in terms of what to protect and how , not how to attack. Anchored in the recognized governance vocabulary: CISA National Critical Functions, the White House June 2025 risk-based assessment and covered-facility guidance, the DOJ/DHS/FAA/FCC legal-advisory structure for detection and mitigation, FAA Remote ID enforcement baseline, DHS S&T resilient-PNT guidance, CISA OT asset-inventory guidance, DOE/EPA continuity and resilience planning materials. The architecture is an event-sourced semantic graph with spatial projections — projections are always recomputable from the underlying graph, so summaries never replace source structure.
fields.detailed_source_mechanism: The architecture is built as four layers running bottom-up, with seven formal doctrines enforced as cross-cutting rules. The doctrines are what stop research drift; the layers are what keep summaries from quietly replacing source.

maintenance_rule: Update this blueprint before strengthening public claims on the website. Do not use word count, module count, or status percentage as a substitute for concrete project substance.
claim_ceiling_rule: Public claims may rise only when the built components, source use, status, and remaining gaps are updated here and reflected in the rendered page.

public_standard_check.Vision: This document supports the public account of Blueprint by preserving the project purpose, reader-facing claim, and professional-portfolio role.
public_standard_check.Mechanism: It connects source material, project components, implementation state, and next actions so the work can be inspected rather than merely asserted.
public_standard_check.What is built: The supporting evidence is the named page, blueprint, packet, project file, source record, or implementation page described in the document.
public_standard_check.Source use: Sources are used as method and source background, domain grounding, or project history; direct quotation and stronger factual claims require passage-level review.
public_standard_check.Plan: Bind claims to exact source anchors, visible implementation evidence, or a narrower public claim.
public_standard_check.Status: Current support record for the professional package unless explicitly marked archival.
public_standard_check.Remaining Gaps: Passage-level source check, implementation verification, citation cleanup, and proportional public wording remain open where evidence is incomplete.

public_pitch.what_it_is: A defensive UAS and critical-infrastructure research observatory built around national critical functions rather than generic drone security.
public_pitch.distinctive_mechanism: It routes malicious-UAS and infrastructure risk through a function-first defensive-research model: CISA National Critical Functions, FAA Remote ID, White House guidance, DOJ/DHS/FAA/FCC legal-advisory constraints, OT asset inventory, DOE/EPA-relevant function dependencies, semantic graphs, provenance vaulting, publication forge outputs, and recomputable projection logic. It also guards against turning defensive analysis into operational attack guidance by filtering timing, payload, and bypass details.
public_pitch.why_it_matters: It matters because critical-infrastructure defense should begin with the function being protected, the legal and operational constraints around intervention, the projection engine that can be recomputed, and the evidence path that supports each protective claim.
public_pitch.pitch_integrity_note: Mechanism-first public pitch inserted after the Nexus Lab mechanism-erasure audit on 2026-05-13; source basis: rendered correction table.

pitch_integrity_audit.standard: object, distinctive mechanism, value, built state, and non-overclaim discipline must be visible.

researcher_process_map.summary: A defensive UAS and critical-infrastructure research observatory built around national critical functions rather than generic drone security.
researcher_process_map.final_use_case: It matters because critical-infrastructure defense should begin with the function being protected, the legal and operational constraints around intervention, the projection engine that can be recomputed, and the evidence path that supports each protective claim.
